_doc: Hand-crafted minimal initial state for the stochastic forced-event
ion-driver cross-reference (CLAUDE.md validation target 5).
The collision KERNEL (impact-parameter sampling, scattering
kinematics, dE/E_0 distribution) is already covered statistically
by scripts/collision_comparison_test/. This task tests the DRIVER
PLUMBING around the kernel:
- probability calculation receives correct per-step inputs,
- velocity-dependent cross section is integrated correctly,
- mass attachment fires after collisions in the right order,
- mass history and energy bookkeeping stay consistent,
- per-step storage records collision/attachment events.
Approach: forced events. Inflated sigma_0 so the per-step Mode-3
probability dr * sigma * rho_droplet is well above 1 every step;
every random draw therefore satisfies trial < p_scatter and a
collision fires. mass_attach_probability = 1 makes every collision
an attachment. Event counts and mass history are therefore
deterministic and identical on both sides without requiring
cross-language RNG identity. Post-collision velocities, energies,
and the mass-attach defect are RNG-dependent and only checked as
PER_SIDE_INVARIANTS, not cross-language.
Initial-state choices:
- atoms deep inside a R = 10 A droplet (depth ~ -7.5 A), so the
atoms remain at depth < 0 for all 5 steps even after Coulomb
acceleration
- moderate v = 3 A/ps so E_kin/atom ~ 0.16 eV stays well above
the Landau cutoff E_min ~ 1 meV throughout (each He collision
removes at most ~12% of E_kin)
- sigma_0 = 1e6 A^2 with exponent -2: sigma(v) = 1e6 / v^2.
At v ~ 3 A/ps -> sigma ~ 1.1e5 A^2; with dr ~ 0.03 A and
rho_droplet = 0.01752 /A^3, p_scatter ~ 58 -- safely above 1.
